title: Israel-Iran war: What US 30-day waiver on Russian oil means for India - explained
content: Around 90% of India’s crude oil is imported - and around 40-50% of India’s crude oil imports come from the Middle East, particularly after it substantially reduced purchases of Russian crude oil.

title: ‘Come of age’: Shastri hails Samson, backs Abhishek ahead of T20 WC final
content: Former India head coach Ravi Shastri lauded Sanju Samson's T20 World Cup 2026 resurgence, highlighting his improved mental fortitude and smarter play. Samson's match-winning knocks, including a semifinal 89, have been pivotal. Shastri also backed struggling opener Abhishek Sharma, urging faith in his abilities for the final against New Zealand.

title: Watch: Israel shares video of precision strikes in Beirut; Hezbollah's military infrastructure hit
content: The Israeli Defense Forces conducted a significant overnight strike in Beirut, targeting Hezbollah's headquarters and military infrastructure within a densely populated area. This action, described as the twenty-sixth wave of strikes, followed Hezbollah's alleged violation of a truce and its involvement in opening a new front in the ongoing conflict.

title: Biggest travel companies react as Altman accepts that ChatGPT can't do everything
content: OpenAI has abandoned plans for direct in-app bookings within ChatGPT, redirecting users to third-party apps instead. This strategic shift, seen as a "stunning admission" by analysts, delays the vision of AI platforms replacing traditional apps. The move acknowledges the operational complexities and liabilities of e-commerce, particularly in sectors like travel.

title: Axar Patel picks tougher of his two ‘stunning’ catches — Watch & choose yours
content: Axar Patel's spectacular fielding heroics were pivotal in India's nail-biting T20 World Cup semifinal win against England.  His incredible diving catch to dismiss Harry Brook and a crucial relay catch to get Will Jacks broke vital partnerships.  Patel deemed the Brook catch tougher, though the Jacks dismissal was more significant for the match's outcome.

title: I am a tribal man, can I seek mutual divorce from my SC wife? Chhattisgarh high court says yes
content: The Chhattisgarh High Court overturned a Family Court's rejection of a mutual-consent divorce petition. The court ruled that Section 2(2) of the Hindu Marriage Act is protective, not exclusionary, allowing a Scheduled Tribe member who married under Hindu customs to seek divorce under the Act. The case was remitted for a decision on merits.

title: Google founder Sergey Brin reportedly linked to Billionaire Bunker
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is reportedly linked to a $51 million waterfront mansion purchase in Miami's exclusive Allison Island. While his name isn't on the deed, transaction documents point to his involvement. This move places him alongside other tech titans like Larry Page and Mark Zuckerberg, who have also invested heavily in Miami's luxury real estate market, driving up prices.

title: Middle East: Emirates, Etihad resume limited services; Indian airlines add extra flights
content: West Asia conflict has grounded thousands of flights across the Middle East, stranding passengers and forcing airlines to reroute or cancel services. Major carriers like Air France, British Airways, and Cathay Pacific have suspended routes. While some Gulf airlines are resuming limited operations, Indian carriers are adding flights to manage demand and repatriation efforts.

title: Rahul takes 'compromised individual' dig at PM after US waiver on Russian oil
content: Rahul Gandhi criticized PM Modi's foreign policy, calling the US waiver on Russian oil purchases a result of exploiting a "compromised individual." The Congress questioned this "American blackmail," while the US stated the waiver aims to stabilize global energy amid Middle East tensions, expecting India to increase US oil imports.

title: Karnataka govt announces social media ban for children under age of 16
content: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah announced plans for a social media ban for children under 16 while presenting the 2026-27 state budget. He also criticized the central government for its alleged injustice to the state and urged greater sensitivity to Karnataka's demands, highlighting the state's leading role in national development and tax contributions.

title: Oracle to start layoffs which may go up to 30K as company faces cash crunch
content: Oracle is reportedly planning significant job cuts, potentially thousands, to manage a growing cash crunch fueled by its massive AI data center expansion. This restructuring, costing over $1.6 billion, aims to address financial pressures from a $300 billion OpenAI partnership. The company's stock has seen a substantial decline as Wall Street scrutinizes its aggressive spending and debt.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2': Ranveer Singh announces trailer launch date and time
content: The highly anticipated 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' trailer drops tomorrow, March 7th, at 11:01 AM, as announced by Ranveer Singh. This espionage thriller, directed by Aditya Dhar, continues the story of Ranveer's character, Hamza Ali Mazari, and features Arjun Rampal as the villain. The film hits cinemas worldwide on March 19, 2026. Read the full article to know everything about the movie in detail.

title: Ukrainian drone targets civilians at grocery store in Russian-occupied Kherson town; two killed
content: A Ukrainian drone attack on Oleshky, a Moscow-occupied town in southern Ukraine, killed two civilians and injured at least 12. The strike reportedly targeted people near a grocery store. In a separate incident in Sevastopol, a downed Ukrainian drone injured nine people, including three children, and caused damage to an apartment building.

title: ‘One more match’: Samson eyes another big knock in India’s summit clash vs NZ
content: Sanju Samson's explosive 89 powered India to the T20 World Cup final against England. This stellar knock marks a significant personal comeback after a period of uncertainty. Samson, who also scored 97 in the previous match, emphasised the importance of patience and focusing on basics, even stepping away from social media to regain his form.

title: Nitish Kumar’s RS nomination: JDU workers vandalise office, allege BJP plot
content: JD(U) workers protested outside CM Nitish Kumar's residence and party office, expressing anger and disbelief over his Rajya Sabha nomination. Alleging a conspiracy and pressure, some threatened self-immolation and vandalized property, demanding to meet their leader and questioning the authenticity of his social media posts.

title: What is THAAD? The US missile defence system with ‘hit-to-kill’ tech playing big role in Iran war
content: Iran's IRGC claims its latest "Operation True Promise-4" attacks bypassed US THAAD missile defence, targeting Israeli sites. Despite leadership losses, Iran continues strikes across the Middle East, straining Western air defence systems like THAAD, Patriot, and Standard Missiles. These vital interceptors are already depleted by aid to Ukraine and Red Sea deployments

title: Middle East crisis: Strait of Hormuz tensions add risks for subsea cables critical to India’s data flows
content: The Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping lane, is now a vulnerable point for undersea internet cables due to escalating West Asian tensions. Disruptions have halted crucial repair operations, impacting internet traffic and India's data center ambitions. This geopolitical flashpoint challenges previous perceptions of the route's safety.

title: Hardik interacts with Dhoni & son Agastya in the stands — video melts hearts
content: India's thrilling T20 World Cup semi-final win over England was marked by Hardik Pandya's stellar all-round performance and a heartwarming off-field moment. A viral video captured MS Dhoni guiding Pandya's son Agastya towards his father, melting hearts online.  The team now faces New Zealand in the final, aiming for a historic title defense.

title: Gulf conflict: London-Hyderavbad direct flight at Rs 2.5L as airfares skyrocket
content: Hyderabadis abroad are facing significantly higher airfares for flights returning home due to disruptions from the US-Iran conflict. Direct flights from London to Hyderabad have reached nearly Rs 2.5 lakh, while fares from Gulf countries have surged by almost 200%. Reduced flight capacity and high demand are contributing to these unprecedented price hikes.

title: Nashik, Gwalior and now Assam: 3rd Sukhoi fighter jet crash in recent years
content: Two pilots were killed after an Sukhoi‑30MKI fighter jet of the Indian Air Force crashed during a training mission in Karbi Anglong, Assam. The aircraft had taken off from Jorhat Air Force Station and lost radar contact at 7.42pm on Thursday. Wreckage was located around 1am on Friday after an overnight search through the hilly terrain.

title: Bengaluru to see taller buildings: What higher FAR norms mean for home investors
content: Bengaluru's real estate market is set for a transformation with the Karnataka government's revised Floor Area Ratio (FAR) guidelines, allowing for significantly taller buildings. This policy aims to promote vertical growth and efficient land utilization, particularly along industrial corridors, fostering economic development and creating new investment opportunities.

title: ED searches multiple sites linked to Anil Ambani’s Reliance Power
content: The Enforcement Directorate conducted searches at multiple locations in Mumbai and Hyderabad linked to Reliance Power as part of a bank fraud and money laundering probe. The investigation also involves suspected financial irregularities under the Foreign Exchange Management Act, with Anil Ambani having been questioned twice previously.

title: Counting begins in Nepal elections: Who are the key faces and what's at stake
content: Nepal is holding crucial parliamentary elections following a youth-led uprising that ousted the previous government. Millions are voting in a bid to combat corruption and unemployment, with a new generation of leaders challenging established parties. The outcome could significantly reshape the Himalayan nation's political future, with coalition governments expected.

title: Sanju Samson reclaims bragging rights against nemesis Jofra Archer
content: Sanju Samson's dropped catch by Harry Brook in the third over proved costly for England. Samson, who was dismissed by Jofra Archer multiple times previously, went on to score a blistering 89 off 42 balls, including 38 runs off 14 deliveries from Archer. His aggressive innings propelled India to a formidable 253/7.

title: Pakistan left with 26 days of fuel, may impose work-from-home as Strait of Hormuz crisis deepens
content: Pakistan is implementing major measures to ensure fuel stability amid trade slowdown due to the Strait of Hormuz closure. Proposals include weekly price revisions, compensation for increased oil company costs, and fuel conservation. The country is exploring alternative import routes and has substantial existing fuel stocks.

title: Iran hints at 'unseen' weapons as Middle East war rages on; US-Israel intensify strikes — key points
content: The Middle East is engulfed in conflict as the US and Israel launch extensive military operations against Iran, following the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.  Retaliatory strikes have been launched by Iran, targeting US bases, while both sides report significant military actions, including the destruction of an Iranian "drone carrier" and damage to schools.

title: Stock market today: Nifty50 opens below 24,650; BSE Sensex down over 500 points
content: Stock market today: After a relief rally on Thursday, Nifty50 and BSE Sensex opened in red on Friday on weak global cues amid escalating Middle East tensions. While Nifty50 went below 24,650, BSE Sensex was down over 500 points.

title: Khamenei killing planned in November 2025? Inside Netanyahu's 'small forum' plot
content: Israel's defence minister Katz revealed a plan to assassinate Ayatollah Khamenei was considered as early as November 2025, with the timeline accelerated due to Iran's anti-regime protests. This led to joint US-Israeli planning and a high-intensity military campaign aimed at dismantling Iran's nuclear and missile capabilities and supporting regional proxies.

title: Major records set in Wankhede thriller as India reach second straight T20 WC final
content: India secured a thrilling seven-run victory against England in the T20 World Cup 2026 semi-final, reaching their second consecutive final. A run-fest saw India post 253/7, with Sanju Samson starring. England's Jacob Bethell's maiden century wasn't enough as India clinched the nail-biting contest, setting up a final against New Zealand.

title: No panic buying yet but evening crowd surges at fuel stations
content: Fuel stations in Kolkata and Howrah experienced unusually long queues as motorists rushed to fill their tanks amid rumours of an impending petrol shortage or price hike. Despite assurances from oil companies about adequate supply, sporadic panic buying occurred during evening peak hours, driven by unverified social media messages.

title: Who bombed Iranian girls’ school? US investigators suspect American forces
content: An Iranian elementary school in Minab was reportedly hit by precision strikes during Operation Epic Fury, killing at least 165 people. US and Israeli forces are suggested as perpetrators, with reports indicating simultaneous attacks on an adjacent IRGC naval base.

title: ‘I made a big mistake ...’: Brook reveals key reason behind England’s heartbreak
content: England captain Harry Brook lamented fielding errors, especially dropping Sanju Samson on 15, as a key reason for their T20 World Cup semi-final loss to India. Samson's 89 powered India to 253, a total England narrowly missed despite Jacob Bethell's stunning 105. Brook praised his team's fight but acknowledged crucial mistakes.

title: Hockey player stabbed to death in Chandigarh over property dispute
content: A 21-year-old state-level hockey player was fatally stabbed by her relatives in Chandigarh over a property dispute. The victim, Taniya, was allegedly attacked by her cousin Krishna and others during an argument. Critically injured, she was declared dead on arrival at the hospital.

title: Married at 12, left for dead at 16: How Kalpana Saroj built a Rs 100-crore empire
content: From a child bride facing abuse and societal stigma at sixteen, Kalpana Saroj's journey is a testament to resilience. Surviving a suicide attempt, she moved to Mumbai, built a furniture business, and ultimately revived the failing Kamani Tubes. Today, she leads a business empire worth over ₹100 crore, redefining success through courage and determination.

title: Rahul Gandhi questions PM Modi's 'silence' on warship sinking in 'our backyard'
content: Congress leaders Rahul Gandhi and Mallikarjun Kharge criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi's silence on the "conflict" and the sinking of an Iranian warship. They accused the government of "surrendering strategic sovereignty" and "undoing" India's foreign policy, particularly concerning oil imports and the safety of Indian citizens in the Gulf region.

title: Over 60% turnout as Nepal votes in 'historic' polls powered by Gen Z
content: Nepal's Gen Z voters turned out in large numbers for snap parliamentary elections, a significant development following September protests that reshaped the country's politics. Despite logistical challenges in the mountainous nation, preliminary turnout exceeded 60%, with young activists viewing election day as the next phase of their movement for change and accountability.

title: T20 WC: ICC arranges charter flight for Windies’ return amid West Asia conflict
content: The West Indies cricket team, stranded in India after the T20 World Cup due to flight disruptions, is expected to fly home on a chartered flight on Friday with ICC assistance. Meanwhile, Zimbabwe has begun returning home in batches via alternate routes, and South Africa's travel arrangements are also being finalised.

title: Absconding doctor taken into custody in Angamaly ‘hit-and-run’ case in Kerala
content: Cyriac George, a native of Athirampuzha in Kottayam, detained from a resort at Wagamon where he was allegedly hiding following the alleged hit-and-run incident in which in which a 19-year-old college student, Jasliya Johnson, was killed on February 28

title: Court orders framing charges against Farooq Abdullah, others in JKCA ‘scam’ case
content: In a five-page order passed earlier this week, Chief Judicial Magistrate of Srinagar, Tabasum, directed that the matter be listed on March 12 for framing of charges

title: Israel-Iran conflict: Kerala CM urges PM Modi to intervene in addressing travel woes of Keralite expatriates
content: Drawing attention to inflated ticket prices charged by airlines, Kerala requests Centre to engage with companies in regulating ticket fares ‘in this hour of distress’

title: Congress demands short duration discussion on West Asia conflict in Parliament
content: Jairam Ramesh claimed that the Modi government today stands "shrunken and diminished" with India's global standing never being "as weak as it is now"

title: Raj Bhavans have become BJP war rooms: TMC slams government over major Governor reshuffle
content: Trinamool Congress leader Sukhendu Sekhar Roy said State governments should be involved in the appointment of Governors

content: Tamil Nadu Governor R.N. Ravi who administered the oath of office also handed over the warrant of appointment issued by President Droupadi Murmu after the Chief Secretary read out the warrant

content: The grief-stricken father occasionally regained his composure to say that Purvesh took immense pride in flying a fighter jet

content: "President Murmu will visit Darjeeling, West Bengal, March 7, where she will grace the 9th International Santal Conference, being organised by the International Santal Council," says Rashtrapati Bhavan

title: Andhra Pradesh keen on establishing world-class theme parks in State, says Tourism Minister Kandula Durgesh
content: Durgesh said the Tourism Department is in talks with companies like Imagica and Wonderla, which operate similar parks in Mumbai, Bengaluru and Hyderabad

title: SpiceJet to operate 14 special flights from UAE on March 6
content: The carrier will operate 9 flights from Fujairah to Mumbai, 4 services to Delhi and 1 from Dubai to Pune, according to a statement

title: Karnataka Budget 2026: Navali Balancing Reservoir mentioned, but project remains on paper
content: Over 30 tmcft silt accumulation in Tungabhadra reservoir prompts Navali balancing reservoir proposal to offset storage loss

title: Rajnath Singh calls for Indian maritime leadership amid West Asia crisis
content: The unfolding developments in West Asia are "highly unusual," and the situation in the region could adversely impact the global economy, says Defence Minister

title: Karnataka Budget proposes new hostels, minority residential schools and education support schemes
content: To meet the growing demand for minority residential education, Chief Minister Siddaramaiah announced in the Budget that ten Sant Shishunala Shariff Residential Schools with a CBSE curriculum will be started

title: Need to make agricultural products export-oriented, globally-competitive: PM Modi
content: PM Modi said the National Mission on Edible Oil and Pulses and the National Mission on Natural Farming are all strengthening the agriculture sector

title: SRMIST introduces AI agent ‘Scout’ for faculty recruitment
content: The platform has provisions for secure facial biometric authentication of candidates, followed by knowledge assessments and conversational AI interviews where questions are generated based on the candidate’s resume and the job requirements, a release said

title: Govt asks refiners to maximise LPG production, OMCs to prioritise supply to domestic consumers
content: The order seeks to prioritise that LPG produced is supplied and marketed solely to consumers of domestic LPG.

title: Karnataka Budget 2026-27: State Government proposes to ban social media for children under 16 years
content: The decision comes after Australia blocked social media, including TikTok, Alphabet’s YouTube and Meta’s Instagram and Facebook, for teens since last December

title: First rock blast for Kerala’s tunnel road project successfully completed under safety measures
content: Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan inaugurates process at Marippuzha in Kozhikode. The proposed tunnel road connects Kozhikode and Wayanad districts

title: Inside Visakhapatnam’s 25-year-old Biodiversity Park
content: As Visakhapatnam’s Biodiversity Park marks 25 years, the green refuge continues to nurture rare plants, wildlife and environmental curiosity in the heart of the city

title: At least one student killed, 11 others injured in ceiling collapse during tuition class in Navi Mumbai
content: At the time of the incident, 11 students were in the room when a portion of the upper floor slab suddenly fractured and fell

title: How an Assam honey business turned to mead
content: AS01 in Assam works with forest beekeepers to create mead infused with local fruits, spices and botanicals

title: Karnataka Budget 2026 focuses on maternal health, infrastructure upgrade and digital health systems
content: Ante-natal and post-natal care services are being strengthened while maternity hospitals are being provided with additional staff, guidance and modern equipment

title: Two siblings on way to exam killed by uncle and cousin over property dispute in Madhya Pradesh
content: Accused Harisingh Malviya and his son allegedly intercepted the siblings on their way to the examination centre and attacked them with sticks, says SP Deepak Shukla

title: Kavinder Gupta: from VHP to Himachal Pradesh Governor
content: A seasoned BJP leader who rose from the ranks of the RSS and went on to hold key constitutional posts, Kavinder Gupta has now been named the new Governor of Himachal Pradesh

title: Karnataka Budget 2026-27: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah presents his 17th budget, with an outlay of ₹4,48,004 crore
content: An expected fall in GST revenue due to rate rationalisation seems to have been countered by an increase in the State’s share of the tax devolution pool, as per recommendations of the 16th Finance Commission

title: Was VSR owner questioned by CID or extended royal hospitality: Rohit Pawar
content: “At night, Singh was even escorted away like a guest in a police vehicle with black-tinted windows,” Rohit Pawar said

title: Budget 2026: Highlights for North Karnataka
content: Medical college in Vijayapura district, and an agriculture college will be set up in Belagavi district

title: Lt. Gen. (Retd) Syed Ata Hasnain: the new Governor of Bihar
content: Lt. General (retired) Syed Ata Hasnain has been appointed to one of the country’s key constitutional positions, bringing with him decades of military leadership and public service.

title: Karnataka Budget 2026: Highlights
content: Karnataka creates unique, strong, sustainable development models, described as 11G model economy, says Siddaramaiah in budget speech

title: ‘Thulasi’ and ‘Ninna Notavu’: A fresh wave in Kannada indie music
content: Kannada indie music is finally getting its moment in the spotlight, with catchy new singles such as ‘Thulasi’ and ‘Ninna Notavu’ striking a chord with the younger crowd

title: NC’s Ruhullah, PDP’s Parra criticise J&K CM over his Iran stand, arrests in Kashmir
content: No NC leader has commented on the J&K Police’s move to lodge a First Information Report (FIR) against Aga Syed Ruhullah for allegedly “creating fear, disturbing public order, and inciting unlawful activities”

title: Iran-Israel War: 50 persons from Karnataka who were stranded in UAE arrive in Delhi
content: The State Government said that the passengers were provided breakfast, and arrangements were made to transfer them to the domestic terminal of Delhi airport from where they would proceed to Bengaluru, Mangaluru and Hubballi

title: Congress fields Anurag Sharma for lone Rajya Sabha seat in Himachal Pradesh
content: Anurag Sharma filed his nomination papers for the Rajya Sabha elections scheduled to be held on March 16

title: Tamil Nadu government formulates SOP for engaging senior counsels to represent it in Supreme Court
content: The SOP has been formulated in view of the increasing instances of engaging Senior Counsels in many cases before the Supreme Court. This is the first time a SOP has been formulated.

title: LeBron bests Kareem for most made FG in history
content: LeBron James made the 15,838th FG of his career on Thursday night, passing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar for the most made field goals in NBA history.

title: Source: DE Hunter gets $40M deal from Texans
content: The Texans agreed to a one-year, $40 million contract extension with defensive end Danielle Hunter, a source confirmed to ESPN.

title: Despite rust, Young dazzles Wiz crowd in debut
content: In his limited time on the court, Trae Young proved in his Wizards debut that he's the type of player the team hasn't had since John Wall, thrilling the D.C. crowd and finishing with 12 points in a 122-112 loss to the Jazz.

title: Ex-Packers prez, team HOFer Harlan dies at 89
content: Former Packers president Bob Harlan, who helped restore the franchise to greatness, died Thursday at the age of 89.

title: Judge rules Beasley owes $1M to former agency
content: A federal judge in New York issued a $1 million civil judgment against NBA free agent Malik Beasley on Thursday in a contract dispute with his former agency.

title: Caps trade vet defenseman Carlson to Ducks
content: In what might be the most surprising development ahead of Friday's NHL trade deadline, John Carlson's time with the Capitals is over.

title: Pens' Malkin ejected for slashing, could face ban
content: Penguins forward Evgeni Malkin received a five-minute major penalty and a game misconduct for slashing the Sabres' Rasmus Dahlin in the head during Thursday's game.

title: Sources: Bears to release veteran LB Edmunds
content: The Bears are releasing linebacker Tremaine Edmunds, a source told ESPN, a move that creates $15 million in cap space for the team.

content: From star sluggers like Ohtani and Judge to top pitchers like Skubal, we rank -- and make predictions for -- the best players in baseball right now.

content: In his 217-minute speech, Mr. Siddaramaiah presented a State Budget with a total outlay pegged at ₹4,48,004 crore.

title: BJP claims ₹39,000 crore of SCSP-TSP funds diverted for guarantee schemes by Congress government in Karnataka
content: BJP leader Rajkumar Patil Telkur claimed that the Siddaramaiah-led government is reportedly preparing to divert an additional ₹15,000 crore in the new budget, continuing the pattern of misallocation

title: India in touch with multilateral agencies, U.S. DFC for shipping insurance, to shield impact on fuel supplies
content: India is in touch with Paris-headquartered International Energy Agency (IEA) and ministers of oil-producing countries, among others, as it looks to keep their fuel supplies secure

title: Hyderabad’s adulterated food raids: Decoy buyers, tip-offs lead to a series of busts
content: The raids have led to the seizure of thousands of kilograms of raw material and hundreds of kilograms of adulterated food products.

title: When gods walk at dawn: A traveller’s guide to experiencing Theyyam in North Kerala
content: In Kavvayi, 40 kilometres from Kannur, a man becomes a ‘god’. For visitors willing to trade sleep for firelight, the Kaliyattam festival at the Sree Kathivanoor Veeran temple offers one of Kerala’s most intense cultural experiences. We travel to show you how and why you should experience this

title: All five Rajya Sabha candidates file nomination in Odisha
content: President of the State unit of Bharatiya Janata Party, Manmohan Samal; sitting BJP Rajya Sabha member, Sujeet Kumar; and hotelier and former Union Coal Minister, Dilip Ray, all backed by the saffron party, submitted their papers.

title: Security at airport takes precedence over devotional right, says Bombay HC
content: The petition was moved by the Taxi-Rickshaw Ola-Uber Men’s Union, which contended that a temporary shed in the airport vicinity, historically used by drivers for congregational prayers, was demolished by civic authorities last year.

title: A Tale of Two Parivars in Bihar
content: With Nitish Kumar set for exile, the Janata Parivar is scuttling towards extinction while the Sangh Parivar is ascendant, and Mandal is now a subset of Kamandal

title: Experiencing heat during pregnancy results in fewer male babies: study
content: Beyond birth ratios, research has linked rising heat to severe maternal health complications, including hypertension and gestational diabetes; researchers emphasise that many global health research agencies underestimate the impact on vulnerable populations

title: Bulldozers on Bhoodan lands kick up political dust
content: Nearly 600 houses were razed in a massive demolition drive at a village near Khammam city on February 24, rendering over 1,800 people homeless. While officials say the action was taken to clear unauthorised structures and reclaim land worth over ₹250 crore, the displaced families, many of whom claim to hold pattas issued by the erstwhile Andhra Pradesh Bhoodan Board in 2014, allege they were not given any notice. The demolitions have sparked protests, sharp political reactions and renewed debate over the fate of lands once donated for the landless under the historic Bhoodan movement, writes P. Sridhar

title: U.S. issues 30-day waiver to allow India to purchase Russian oil amid West Asian supply woes
content: Scott Bessent said the move aims to enable oil to keep flowing into the global market amid the war in West Asia that has disrupted oil supply

title: Why north Karnataka’s under-nutrition burden stays high
content: In north Karnataka districts, child undernutrition has been an unrelenting problem for decades. More than a function of food scarcity, it is an outcome of overlapping issues, some inter-generational and some structural, writes Shilpa Elizabeth

title: The Kumbakonam connection of MGR, M.K. Nambyar and M.S.  Swaminathan
content: The families of the iconic film star and former Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, the distinguished constitutional lawyer, and the celebrated agricultural scientist hailed from Kerala. But they spent a formative part of their lives in Kumbakonam, known for its rich cultural heritage, before rising to national prominence in politics, cinema, law, and science

title: A trailblazer for the rights of  Dalits and the empowerment of women
content: Meenambal Sivaraj was born on December 12, 1904, in Rangoon, in a family that placed great importance on education and service. One of her most significant contributions was her work with All India Scheduled Castes Federation founded by  Ambedkar

title: Bhaktavatsalam’s take on the destruction of documents relating to anti-Hindi agitation
content: He said he was advised that papers on politicians against whom detention orders had been served need not be kept so that they would not be available to the very people who were likely to take charge of the government. When Ministers came to know about officers who had made reports about them, they would get prejudiced, he further said

title: Suspension of flights, shipping surcharge push exporters from south India to the edge
content: Suspension of flights from India to West Asia disrupts supply chain for vegetables, fruits, and seafood exported from South India

title: Ashok Gehlot slams Centre’s silence on U.S. attack on Iranian warship in Indian Ocean
content: An emerging superpower should not remain a mute spectator to such violent incidents in its own region, says Mr. Gehlot; if we are the true protectors of the Indian Ocean, we must put our autonomy and safety of our guests above all else, he adds

title: Matuas pushed into ‘confusing situation’, deliberately excluded from voter list by SIR, says Mamata
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee alleges that political games are being played in the name of giving citizenship and questions are being raised about their identity

title: The promise of a bridge to development in Odisha’s Kalahandi
content: For tribal and Dalit families stranded on a landmass within the vast Indravati reservoir in Odisha’s Kalahandi district, life remains in limbo. Satyasundar Barik travels to this remote, rarely visited region to examine how a project widely credited with ushering in prosperity has failed to positively transform the lives of the people there

title: Nitish Kumar files nomination papers for Rajya Sabha, ending 20 years as Bihar CM
content: Confirming speculation about the end of his 20-year rule in Bihar with a post on social media, Mr. Kumar declared that he had a “desire in his heart” to become a member of both the Houses of Parliament.

title: Kalyan Singh told an ‘utter lie’: K.K. Venugopal on Babri Masjid demolition
content: In a candid conversation with The Hindu, K.K. Venugopal discusses the “command from political masters” that triggered the anti-Sikh riots in the 1980s, his sense of betrayal when he realised then-UP CM Kalyan Singh had told him an “utter lie” that the Babri Masjid would not be demolished in the 1990s.

title: Kerala’s public health sector in need of a booster shot
content: Complaints of lapses in the famed public health system of Kerala hog the headlines as the State braces for Assembly polls. C. Maya finds out what is ailing the health sector of the State

title: Rajya Sabha election: Congress fields new face Christopher in Tamil Nadu
content: The 53-year-old is one of the secretaries of the All India Congress Committee, and had been in charge of northeastern States such as Manipur and Nagaland

title: The tumultuous tenure of R.N. Ravi as Tamil Nadu Governor
content: From regularly walking out of the Assembly to raising issues over the appointment of Vice-Chancellors to State-run universities, Ravi and the DMK government led by Chief Minister M.K. Stalin were rarely on the same page

title: KWA to enhance efficiency of water treatment plant in Aluva
content: The upgrade to result in generation of additional 10 MLD of treated water and improvement of drinking water supply in Kochi and adjoining panchayats

title: Internal quota issue hangs fire with discussion incomplete in Cabinet
content: Siddaramaiah told his colleagues that a decision could be taken at a special cabinet that would be called shortly

title: Governor removes Vice-Chancellor of Sree Sankaracharya University of Sanskrit
content: Ciza Thomas, Vice-Chancellor of APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University, will hold additional charge of the Vice-Chancellor of the university

title: Probe Agency Raids Locations Connected To Reliance Power In Mumbai, Hyderabad

title: Nepal Election 2026 Results Live Updates: Balen Shah's Rastriya Swatantra Party Leads In 68 Seats
content: Nepal General Election 2026 Results Live: The newly formed Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP), led by former rapper Balendra Shah, was leading in the early counting of votes on Friday in Nepal's first general elections since the violent Gen Z protests

title: Panel Reviewing Trump's White House Ballroom Project Gets Earful From Public Opposing It
content: Trump's proposed White House ballroom is "ugly," "grossly out of scale" and a "gold gilded edifice to one man's ego," according to members of the public who got a chance to speak their minds  to a federal panel that's reviewing it.

title: Opinion: Hormuz Is The Hidden Risk To The AI Economy
content: A world where we can cook up AI videos in seconds from the apps on our phones might seem remote from the physical realities of warfare in the seaways of the Persian Gulf. In fact, they're closely intertwined.

title: Iranian Jets Shot Down Two Minutes Before Hitting Targets In Qatar: Report

title: A Blue Sparrow Left Earth's Atmosphere, Returned As Khamenei Executioner

title: Opinion: Opinion | Will The Iran War Force Pakistan To Pick 'Sides'?
content: Given its economic dependence on Gulf countries and strategic leanings toward the US, Pakistan will aim to contain any overt anti-American positioning in public protest or state rhetoric.

content: Zahra Haddad Adel was the daughter of Gholam-Ali Haddad-Adel, a prominent conservative politician and former Speaker of the Iranian Parliament.

title: As War Rages In Gulf, Indians Move Their Money From Dubai To Singapore
content: Soon after the first Iranian missile and drone attacks on Dubai last week, two Indian entrepreneurs based there tried to move more than $100,000 each from their local bank accounts to Singapore to hedge risk.

title: 36 Hours, 3,000 Guided Munitions: How US-Israel Rained Fire On Iran
content: The first 36 hours of the US and Israeli attack against Iran used over 3,000 precision-guided munitions and interceptors, according to a new analysis.

title: Delhi Man Killed As 2 Families Clash After Water Balloon Hits Woman On Holi

title: Iran War Pushes India's Crude Oil Basket To $85/barrel, A 19-month High
content: The price of the Indian basket of crude oil rising to $85.24 per barrel on March 4, the highest level since July 2024.

title: Army To Hire 800 Porters In Rajouri, Poonch For Its Newly Raised Company
content: According to an official advertisement, the 279 Porter Company is being raised for 179 days under the Ministry of Defence.

title: Siblings Headed For Board Exams Beaten To Death In Ambush By Uncle
content: In a chilling incident that has stunned Madhya Pradesh, a brother and sister carrying their exam admit cards were brutally murdered on their way to the examination centre on Friday morning in Dharampuri village of Sehore district.

title: "Do We Need US' Permission?" Opposition Jabs Centre Over Russian Oil Waiver
content: The US said it was issuing a temporary 30-day waiver to Indian refiners to purchase Russian oil amid a raging war in the Middle East that has hit the global supply.

title: Fast break: Who should you pick up before the playoffs begin?
content: the fantasy basketball playoffs are almost here, and context matters more than talent . teams chasing seeding are far more likely to lean heavily on their core players than those playing out the string . goal is not to build the flashiest roster, but to build most reliable one . prioritize players with secure roles and motivated teams .
